broader_audit_recursive_scan.scope: initialize + tools/list responses, recursive walk of every object key at every depth
broader_audit_recursive_scan.exclusion_zone: JSON Schema property declarations under inputSchema.properties — these are user-defined argument names, not MCP-defined response struct fields; renaming them would break tools/call argument extraction in handlers (verified at crates/tldr-mcp/src/tools/ast.rs:21,62,90 etc.)
broader_audit_recursive_scan.result: zero violations after the protocol_version + server_info renames; tools/list was already clean (its only response-struct snake_case fields list_changed/is_error/input_schema were renamed in v0.1.6 work)

clippy_clean.notes: Two clippy lints fired in the new test file during initial implementation (search_is_some, manual_contains) — both refactored to use idiomatic constructs (Iterator::any then slice::contains). No #[allow] suppressions used. Final clippy run: clean.

serde_renames_added.rationale: MCP 2024-11-05 spec wire format; Issue #19 root cause
MCP 2024-11-05 spec wire format; Issue #19 root cause

broader_audit_outcome: literal triage line was sufficient — only InitializeResult had unrenamed snake_case fields on the Serialize side; ToolsListResult, ToolDefinition (input_schema), ToolsCallResult (is_error), ToolsCapability (list_changed), ContentItem (content_type → 'type') already had their renames in v0.1.6 work

scope_decisions.InitializeParams_and_ClientInfo_left_unc: These are Deserialize-only structs (incoming params); the contract VAL-004 scope is response wire format (Serialize side). The current code uses .and_then(|p| from_value(p.clone()).ok()) which silently ignores deser failures, so the missing renames here cause only loss of an eprintln client-info log, not a wire-format failure. Out of scope for VAL-004 — flag for v0.2.2 review.
scope_decisions.JSON_Schema_property_keys_left_unchanged: Under tools/list response, every snake_case key (exclude_hidden, max_results, base_path, entry_points, context_lines, top_k, entry_point, include_docstrings, changed_files, smell_type, include_halstead, entropy_threshold, include_test, severity_filter, vuln_type) appears inside an inputSchema.properties JSON Schema object. These are user-defined argument names extracted by tool handlers via get_optional_*(args, "<exact_key>") — renaming them would break the tools/call argument-passing contract. The MCP wire-format requirement applies to MCP-defined message field names, not to JSON Schema property declarations contained within an inputSchema value. The recursive scan in the test explicitly skips these (see collect_keys helper in mcp_camelcase_test.rs).
